text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Pipes' in your account?
What is the primary benefit of 'Snowpipe'?

options.a: It is faster than the COPY command.
options.b: It provides near real-time data ingestion from cloud storage.
options.c: It does not require a Warehouse.

text: True or False: A 'Task' can be part of a 'Directed Acyclic Graph' (DAG) of tasks.
Which view provides history of all data loads performed using the COPY command in the last 64 days?
What happens if a Virtual Warehouse is 'Suspended' and a new query is submitted to it?

options.b: The warehouse automatically resumes (if auto-resume is ON) and then runs the query.
options.c: The query is queued until a user manually starts the warehouse.
options.d: The query is moved to a different warehouse.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Policy Tags' to manage data masking policies.
Which command allows you to view the privileges granted to a specific user?
What is the impact of 'Auto-clustering' on table availability?

options.a: The table is locked while clustering.
options.b: The table remains available for querying and DML during the process.
options.c: The table is read-only during clustering.

text: True or False: Snowflake 'Stored Procedures' can be written in Javascript.
Which Snowflake edition is required for 'Multi-cluster Warehouse' support?
True or False: Snowflake supports 'External Functions' which allow calling an API outside Snowflake.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Stages'?
What is the maximum file size recommended for data loading in Snowflake?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Row Access Policies' to filter rows based on user role.
Which view provides history of all 'Task' executions in an account?
What is the default T-shirt size for a new Virtual Warehouse if not specified?
Which command is used to see the 'Clustering Depth' of a table?
What is the purpose of 'Dynamic Data Masking'?

options.b: To obscure sensitive data in query results based on the user's role/permissions.

text: True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the individual Warehouse level.
Which Snowflake interface is a command-line tool?
What does 'DML' stand for in Snowflake?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Tri-Secret Secure' for customer-managed encryption keys.
Which command is used to see the 'DDL' of an existing Snowflake table?
True or False: Snowflake's metadata layer is stored in the same storage layer as the user data.
Which function is used to convert a delimited string into an array?
What is the primary benefit of the 'Standard' scaling policy for multi-cluster warehouses?

options.a: It minimizes the number of clusters to save cost.
options.b: It prioritizes query performance by starting new clusters immediately when queries are queued.
options.c: It waits for 2 minutes before starting a new cluster.
options.d: It scales up the size of the cluster rather than adding new ones.

text: True or False: When a database is cloned, the data is duplicated immediately in the storage layer.
Which command is used to download files from an internal stage to a local directory?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention for a Temporary table?
Which view should you query to see the current credits used by a specific Snowflake object tag?
True or False: You can use the 'SYSTEM$EXPLAIN_PLAN_JSON' function to see a query's plan in JSON format.
What is the result of using a 'Clustering Key' on a very small table (e.g., < 1GB)?

options.b: No noticeable benefit; may actually increase cost due to re-clustering overhead.
options.d: The table will be compressed more efficiently.

text: Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization' cost?

options.a: It is included in the Standard edition price.
options.b: It incurs credit costs for both the initial build and continuous maintenance of the search access path.
options.c: It only costs credits when a query actually uses it.
options.d: It is a one-time flat fee.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows 'Database Replication' across different cloud providers (e.g., from AWS to Azure).
What is the primary use of a 'Directory Table'?

options.a: To manage the list of all users in the account.
options.b: To catalog and query metadata about unstructured files in a stage.

text: Which function provides the current timestamp in Snowflake?
True or False: A 'Transient' table has a Fail-safe period of 1 day.
Which view provides the most detail about why a specific query failed?
What is the primary benefit of 'Snowpark' for Data Engineers?

options.a: It allows them to run SQL queries faster.
options.b: It allows them to build complex data transformations using imperative languages like Python/Scala instead of just SQL.
options.d: It provides a new UI for managing databases.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required for 'Database Replication' across accounts?
True or False: Snowflake's 'Auto-Clustering' service will automatically cluster a table if you have defined a clustering key.
Which component of the Snowflake architecture ensures data consistency and ACID compliance?
What does the 'STRIP_OUTER_ARRAY' option do when loading JSON?

options.a: It removes the first and last characters of the file.
options.b: It tells Snowflake to ignore the outer square brackets of a JSON array and load the elements as individual rows.

text: True or False: A 'Share' object can be created using the 'SYSADMIN' role.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Resource Monitors'?
What is the primary purpose of 'Object Tagging'?

options.b: To apply metadata to objects for governance, compliance, and cost tracking.
options.c: To group tables into a cluster.
options.d: To hide objects from specific users.

text: True or False: Snowflake 'External Tables' support both 'Time Travel' and 'Fail-safe'.
Which function allows you to get the DDL of a Snowflake object?
What is the maximum number of days for 'Fail-safe' retention?
Which role can manage 'Network Policies' in Snowflake?

options.c: ACCOUNTADMIN (or those with the appropriate global privilege)

text: Which of the following describes the Snowflake 'Storage' layer?

options.a: It uses a proprietary, encrypted, and compressed columnar format.
options.b: It stores data in standard CSV files.
options.c: It is managed by the customer in their own cloud storage.
options.d: It is stored on the local disks of the virtual warehouses.

text: True or False: When you resize a Warehouse, it affects the currently running queries.
Which command would you use to see if a query used 'Pruning' effectively?

options.b: View the 'Query Profile' in the Snowflake UI

text: What is the result of 'Scale Out' for a Warehouse?

options.a: Increasing the size of the cluster.
options.b: Adding more clusters to a multi-cluster warehouse.
options.c: Moving data to more storage buckets.

text: True or False: You can share data from a 'Transient' table.
Which command is used to remove a file from a stage?
What is the primary role of 'Accountadmin'?

options.b: The highest-level role with full control over the account and billing.

text: True or False: Snowflake credits are billed for the time a Warehouse is in the 'Provisioning' state.
Which component of the query profile shows the time spent reading data from the storage layer?
What happens if a 'Task' is still running when its next scheduled time arrives?

options.a: A second instance of the task starts.
options.b: The next scheduled execution is skipped.
options.d: The task is moved to a priority queue.

text: True or False: You can set 'Object Tags' on columns in a table.
Which role is recommended for creating and managing common objects like databases and warehouses?
What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.b: To restrict which rows of data a user can see based on their role/attributes.

text: True or False: Snowflake handles the maintenance and patching of the underlying cloud servers automatically.
Which command is used to see the list of 'User Roles' in an account?
What is 'Zero-copy cloning'?

options.a: A way to duplicate data without using any storage space initially by using metadata pointers.
options.b: A way to copy data into a different cloud provider for free.
options.d: A way to create an empty table with the same schema.

text: True or False: All Snowflake users have the 'PUBLIC' role by default.
Which layer of the Snowflake architecture stores the result cache?
